message.content:
Implement the following plan:

# Plan: Auto-updating Code Coverage Badge via CI

## Context

The project has no code coverage reporting. The user wants coverage surfaced through the CI pipeline with an auto-updated badge in the README. The repo already uses `Schneegans/dynamic-badges-action@v1.7.0` + a GitHub Gist for the "AI Co-Authored" badge (`.github/workflows/ai-badge.yml`), so we replicate the same pattern.

## Changes

### 1. Update `.github/workflows/ci.yml` — add coverage step to backend job

In the existing `backend` job, change the Test step to generate a coverage profile and then add a step that parses the total coverage % and updates a gist via `Schneegans/dynamic-badges-action`. Only update the badge on pushes to main (not on PRs).

```yaml
- name: Test
  run: go test -coverprofile=coverage.out ./...

- name: Coverage badge
  if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && github.event_name == 'push'
  uses: Schneegans/dynamic-badges-action@v1.7.0
  with:
    auth: ${{ secrets.GIST_SECRET }}
    gistID: 94cc8f0ddf90bb1d04dafbb76102786d   # same gist as AI badge
    filename: coverage.json
    label: coverage
    message: <parsed-pct>%
    valColorRange: <parsed-pct>
    minColorRange: 20
    maxColorRange: 80
```

Coverage % is extracted with `go tool cover -func=coverage.out | grep total | awk '{print $3}'`.

### 2. Update `README.md` — add coverage badge

Add on line 5 (next to existing badges):

```markdown
![Coverage](https://img.shields.io/endpoint?url=https://gist.githubusercontent.com/Poytr1/94cc8f0ddf90bb1d04dafbb76102786d/raw/coverage.json)
```

### 3. Add `coverage` target to `Makefile`

For local use:

```makefile
coverage:
	go test -coverprofile=coverage.out ./...
	go tool cover -func=coverage.out | grep total
```

## Files modified

- `.github/workflows/ci.yml` — add coverage profile + badge update step
- `README.md` — add coverage badge
- `Makefile` — add `coverage` target

## Verification

1. `make coverage` locally — should print total coverage %
2. Push to main — CI backend job should update gist, badge should reflect coverage


If you need specific details from before exiting plan mode (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: /Users/pc/.claude/projects/-Users-pc-web3-ReleaseBeacon/ab8c6970-cff3-401d-9ea5-a3be38a8c460.jsonl

planContent:
# Plan: Auto-updating Code Coverage Badge via CI

## Context

The project has no code coverage reporting. The user wants coverage surfaced through the CI pipeline with an auto-updated badge in the README. The repo already uses `Schneegans/dynamic-badges-action@v1.7.0` + a GitHub Gist for the "AI Co-Authored" badge (`.github/workflows/ai-badge.yml`), so we replicate the same pattern.

## Changes

### 1. Update `.github/workflows/ci.yml` — add coverage step to backend job

In the existing `backend` job, change the Test step to generate a coverage profile and then add a step that parses the total coverage % and updates a gist via `Schneegans/dynamic-badges-action`. Only update the badge on pushes to main (not on PRs).

```yaml
- name: Test
  run: go test -coverprofile=coverage.out ./...

- name: Coverage badge
  if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && github.event_name == 'push'
  uses: Schneegans/dynamic-badges-action@v1.7.0
  with:
    auth: ${{ secrets.GIST_SECRET }}
    gistID: 94cc8f0ddf90bb1d04dafbb76102786d   # same gist as AI badge
    filename: coverage.json
    label: coverage
    message: <parsed-pct>%
    valColorRange: <parsed-pct>
    minColorRange: 20
    maxColorRange: 80
```

Coverage % is extracted with `go tool cover -func=coverage.out | grep total | awk '{print $3}'`.

### 2. Update `README.md` — add coverage badge

Add on line 5 (next to existing badges):

```markdown
![Coverage](https://img.shields.io/endpoint?url=https://gist.githubusercontent.com/Poytr1/94cc8f0ddf90bb1d04dafbb76102786d/raw/coverage.json)
```

### 3. Add `coverage` target to `Makefile`

For local use:

```makefile
coverage:
	go test -coverprofile=coverage.out ./...
	go tool cover -func=coverage.out | grep total
```

## Files modified

- `.github/workflows/ci.yml` — add coverage profile + badge update step
- `README.md` — add coverage badge
- `Makefile` — add `coverage` target

## Verification

1. `make coverage` locally — should print total coverage %
2. Push to main — CI backend job should update gist, badge should reflect coverage

file.content:
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, master]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main, master]

jobs:
  backend:
    name: Backend
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: actions/setup-go@v5
        with:
          go-version-file: go.mod

      - name: Vet
        run: go vet ./...

      - name: Build
        run: go build -o changelogue ./cmd/server

      - name: Test
        run: go test ./...

  frontend:
    name: Frontend
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    defaults:
      run:
        working-directory: web
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: npm
          cache-dependency-path: web/package-lock.json

      - name: Install
        run: npm ci

      - name: Lint
        run: npx eslint

      - name: Build
        run: npm run build

  integration:
    name: Integration
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: actions/setup-go@v5
        with:
          go-version-file: go.mod

      - name: Run integration tests
        run: bash scripts/integration-test.sh

file.content:
.PHONY: up down db-reset build run dev test vet lint \
        frontend-install frontend-dev frontend-build \
        integration-test agent-dev clean

# --- Configuration ---
DATABASE_URL ?= postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/changelogue?sslmode=disable
LISTEN_ADDR  ?= :8080
BINARY       := changelogue

# --- Infrastructure ---
up:
	docker compose up -d
	@echo "Waiting for Postgres to be healthy..."
	@until docker compose exec -T postgres pg_isready -U postgres >/dev/null 2>&1; do sleep 1; done
	@echo "Postgres is ready."

down:
	docker compose down

db-reset:
	docker compose exec -T postgres psql -U postgres -c "DROP DATABASE IF EXISTS changelogue;"
	docker compose exec -T postgres psql -U postgres -c "CREATE DATABASE changelogue;"
	@echo "Database reset."

# --- Backend ---
build:
	go build -o $(BINARY) ./cmd/server

run: build
	DATABASE_URL="$(DATABASE_URL)" LISTEN_ADDR="$(LISTEN_ADDR)" NO_AUTH=true ./$(BINARY)

test:
	go test ./...

vet:
	go vet ./...

lint: vet

# --- Agent Dev ---
agent-dev:
	@if [ -z "$(PROJECT_ID)" ]; then echo "error: PROJECT_ID is required. Usage: make agent-dev PROJECT_ID=<uuid>"; exit 1; fi
	DATABASE_URL="$(DATABASE_URL)" go run ./cmd/agent --project-id=$(PROJECT_ID) web api webui

# --- Frontend ---
frontend-install:
	cd web && npm install

frontend-dev:
	cd web && npm run dev

frontend-build:
	cd web && npm run build

# --- Integration ---
integration-test:
	bash scripts/integration-test.sh

# --- Convenience ---
dev: up run

clean:
	rm -f $(BINARY)
	docker compose down -v

4→ 5→![Build](https://github.com/sentioxyz/releaseguard/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg) 6→![AI Co-Authored](https://img.shields.io/endpoint?url=https://gist.githubusercontent.com/Poytr1/94cc8f0ddf90bb1d04dafbb76102786d/raw/ai-commits.json) 7→ 8→## What it does 9→ 10→- **Discovers releases** by polling Docker Hub, GitHub, ECR Public, GitLab, and PyPI on configurable intervals 11→- **Routes notifications** to Slack, Discord, email, and webhooks the moment a new version lands 12→- **Generates AI reports** via Google Gemini agents that research changelogs, assess risk, and summarize what changed 13→- **Serves a dashboard** (Next.js) for managing projects, sources, subscriptions, and browsing releases in real time 14→ 15→## Architecture 16→ 17→```mermaid 18→graph LR 19→ A["Ingestion
(polling)"] --> B["PostgreSQL +
River Queue"] 20→ B --> C["Agent
(ADK-Go)"] 21→ C --> D["Routing
(channels)"] 22→ 23→ A -.- A1["Docker Hub · GitHub
ECR · GitLab · PyPI"] 24→ B -.- B1["Transactional Outbox
LISTEN/NOTIFY → SSE"] 25→ C -.- C1["Gemini LLM
research tools"] 26→ D -.- D1["Slack · Discord
Email · Webhooks"] 27→``` 28→ 29→Release insert and job enqueue happen in a single SQL transaction — zero-loss guarantee. 30→ 31→See [ARCH.md](ARCH.md), [API.md](API.md), and [DESIGN.md](DESIGN.md) for the full design. 32→ 33→## Quick start 34→ 35→**Prerequisites:** Go 1.25+, Docker, Node.js 20+ 36→ 37→```bash 38→# Start Postgres and the server (NO_AUTH mode) 39→make dev 40→ 41→# In another terminal — start the frontend 42→make frontend-install 43→make frontend-dev 44→``` 45→ 46→The API runs on `localhost:8080`, the dashboard on `localhost:3000`. 47→ 48→## Environment variables 49→ 50→| Variable | Default | Purpose | 51→|----------|---------|---------| 52→| `DATABASE_URL` | `postgres://localhost:5432/changelogue?sslmode=disable` | PostgreSQL connection | 53→| `LISTEN_ADDR` | `:8080` | HTTP server bind address | 54→| `NO_AUTH` | _(unset)_ | Set to `true` to disable API key auth (development) | 55→| `LOG_LEVEL` | `info` | Log level: `debug`, `info`, `warn`, `error` | 56→| `LLM_PROVIDER` | `gemini` | LLM provider: `gemini` or `openai` | 57→| `LLM_MODEL` | `gemini-2.5-flash` / `gpt-5.2` | Model name (default depends on provider) | 58→| `GOOGLE_API_KEY` | _(empty)_ | Gemini API key (required when `LLM_PROVIDER=gemini`) | 59→| `OPENAI_API_KEY` | _(empty)_ | OpenAI API key (required when `LLM_PROVIDER=openai`) | 60→| `OPENAI_BASE_URL` | `https://api.openai.com/v1` | OpenAI-compatible API base URL | 61→ 62→## Project structure 63→ 64→``` 65→cmd/ 66→ server/ Entry point — wires all layers together 67→ agent/ Agent CLI — run agent analysis for a project 68→internal/ 69→ agent/ ADK-Go agent orchestrator, tools, and worker 70→ openai/ OpenAI-compatible LLM provider 71→ api/ REST API, SSE, middleware, auth 72→ db/ Connection pool and migrations 73→ ingestion/ Polling sources (Docker Hub, GitHub, ECR Public, GitLab, PyPI) 74→ models/ Shared domain types 75→ queue/ River job definitions and client 76→ routing/ Notification channels and delivery worker 77→web/ Next.js dashboard (React + Tailwind + shadcn) 78→scripts/ Integration test harness 79→``` 80→ 81→## Extending 82→ 83→More providers (npm, Helm, etc.) and channels (PagerDuty, etc.) are planned. Adding one is a single-interface implementation: 84→ 85→**Add a registry provider** — implement `IIngestionSource` in `internal/ingestion/source.go`: 86→ 87→```go 88→type IIngestionSource interface { 89→ FetchNewReleases(ctx context.Context) ([]IngestionResult, error) 90→} 91→``` 92→ 93→**Add a notification channel** — implement `Sender` in `internal/routing/sender.go`: 94→ 95→```go 96→type Sender interface { 97→ Send(ctx context.Context, channel models.NotificationChannel, msg Message) error 98→} 99→``` 100→ 101→## Useful commands 102→ 103→```bash 104→make build # go build -o changelogue ./cmd/server 105→make test # go test ./... 106→make vet # go vet ./... 107→make lint # alias for vet 108→make integration-test # full integration test (spins up its own Postgres) 109→make db-reset # drop and recreate the local database 110→make frontend-build # build Next.js static export 111→make agent-dev # run agent CLI for a specific project 112→make clean # remove binary, stop containers, delete volumes 113→``` 114→ Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.

file.content:
![Build](https://github.com/sentioxyz/releaseguard/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg) ![AI Co-Authored](https://img.shields.io/endpoint?url=https://gist.githubusercontent.com/Poytr1/94cc8f0ddf90bb1d04dafbb76102786d/raw/ai-commits.json) ## What it does - **Discovers releases** by polling Docker Hub, GitHub, ECR Public, GitLab, and PyPI on configurable intervals - **Routes notifications** to Slack, Discord, email, and webhooks the moment a new version lands - **Generates AI reports** via Google Gemini agents that research changelogs, assess risk, and summarize what changed - **Serves a dashboard** (Next.js) for managing projects, sources, subscriptions, and browsing releases in real time ## Architecture ```mermaid graph LR A["Ingestion
(polling)"] --> B["PostgreSQL +
River Queue"] B --> C["Agent
(ADK-Go)"] C --> D["Routing
(channels)"] A -.- A1["Docker Hub · GitHub
ECR · GitLab · PyPI"] B -.- B1["Transactional Outbox
LISTEN/NOTIFY → SSE"] C -.- C1["Gemini LLM
research tools"] D -.- D1["Slack · Discord
Email · Webhooks"] ``` Release insert and job enqueue happen in a single SQL transaction — zero-loss guarantee. See [ARCH.md](ARCH.md), [API.md](API.md), and [DESIGN.md](DESIGN.md) for the full design. ## Quick start **Prerequisites:** Go 1.25+, Docker, Node.js 20+ ```bash # Start Postgres and the server (NO_AUTH mode) make dev # In another terminal — start the frontend make frontend-install make frontend-dev ``` The API runs on `localhost:8080`, the dashboard on `localhost:3000`. ## Environment variables | Variable | Default | Purpose | |----------|---------|---------| | `DATABASE_URL` | `postgres://localhost:5432/changelogue?sslmode=disable` | PostgreSQL connection | | `LISTEN_ADDR` | `:8080` | HTTP server bind address | | `NO_AUTH` | _(unset)_ | Set to `true` to disable API key auth (development) | | `LOG_LEVEL` | `info` | Log level: `debug`, `info`, `warn`, `error` | | `LLM_PROVIDER` | `gemini` | LLM provider: `gemini` or `openai` | | `LLM_MODEL` | `gemini-2.5-flash` / `gpt-5.2` | Model name (default depends on provider) | | `GOOGLE_API_KEY` | _(empty)_ | Gemini API key (required when `LLM_PROVIDER=gemini`) | | `OPENAI_API_KEY` | _(empty)_ | OpenAI API key (required when `LLM_PROVIDER=openai`) | | `OPENAI_BASE_URL` | `https://api.openai.com/v1` | OpenAI-compatible API base URL | ## Project structure ``` cmd/ server/ Entry point — wires all layers together agent/ Agent CLI — run agent analysis for a project internal/ agent/ ADK-Go agent orchestrator, tools, and worker openai/ OpenAI-compatible LLM provider api/ REST API, SSE, middleware, auth db/ Connection pool and migrations ingestion/ Polling sources (Docker Hub, GitHub, ECR Public, GitLab, PyPI) models/ Shared domain types queue/ River job definitions and client routing/ Notification channels and delivery worker web/ Next.js dashboard (React + Tailwind + shadcn) scripts/ Integration test harness ``` ## Extending More providers (npm, Helm, etc.) and channels (PagerDuty, etc.) are planned. Adding one is a single-interface implementation: **Add a registry provider** — implement `IIngestionSource` in `internal/ingestion/source.go`: ```go type IIngestionSource interface { FetchNewReleases(ctx context.Context) ([]IngestionResult, error) } ``` **Add a notification channel** — implement `Sender` in `internal/routing/sender.go`: ```go type Sender interface { Send(ctx context.Context, channel models.NotificationChannel, msg Message) error } ``` ## Useful commands ```bash make build # go build -o changelogue ./cmd/server make test # go test ./... make vet # go vet ./... make lint # alias for vet make integration-test # full integration test (spins up its own Postgres) make db-reset # drop and recreate the local database make frontend-build # build Next.js static export make agent-dev # run agent CLI for a specific project make clean # remove binary, stop containers, delete volumes ```

name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, master]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main, master]

jobs:
  backend:
    name: Backend
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: actions/setup-go@v5
        with:
          go-version-file: go.mod

      - name: Vet
        run: go vet ./...

      - name: Build
        run: go build -o changelogue ./cmd/server

      - name: Test
        run: go test ./...

  frontend:
    name: Frontend
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    defaults:
      run:
        working-directory: web
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: npm
          cache-dependency-path: web/package-lock.json

      - name: Install
        run: npm ci

      - name: Lint
        run: npx eslint

      - name: Build
        run: npm run build

  integration:
    name: Integration
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: actions/setup-go@v5
        with:
          go-version-file: go.mod

      - name: Run integration tests
        run: bash scripts/integration-test.sh

toolUseResult.originalFile:
![Build](https://github.com/sentioxyz/releaseguard/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg) ![AI Co-Authored](https://img.shields.io/endpoint?url=https://gist.githubusercontent.com/Poytr1/94cc8f0ddf90bb1d04dafbb76102786d/raw/ai-commits.json) ## What it does - **Discovers releases** by polling Docker Hub, GitHub, ECR Public, GitLab, and PyPI on configurable intervals - **Routes notifications** to Slack, Discord, email, and webhooks the moment a new version lands - **Generates AI reports** via Google Gemini agents that research changelogs, assess risk, and summarize what changed - **Serves a dashboard** (Next.js) for managing projects, sources, subscriptions, and browsing releases in real time ## Architecture ```mermaid graph LR A["Ingestion
(polling)"] --> B["PostgreSQL +
River Queue"] B --> C["Agent
(ADK-Go)"] C --> D["Routing
(channels)"] A -.- A1["Docker Hub · GitHub
ECR · GitLab · PyPI"] B -.- B1["Transactional Outbox
LISTEN/NOTIFY → SSE"] C -.- C1["Gemini LLM
research tools"] D -.- D1["Slack · Discord
Email · Webhooks"] ``` Release insert and job enqueue happen in a single SQL transaction — zero-loss guarantee. See [ARCH.md](ARCH.md), [API.md](API.md), and [DESIGN.md](DESIGN.md) for the full design. ## Quick start **Prerequisites:** Go 1.25+, Docker, Node.js 20+ ```bash # Start Postgres and the server (NO_AUTH mode) make dev # In another terminal — start the frontend make frontend-install make frontend-dev ``` The API runs on `localhost:8080`, the dashboard on `localhost:3000`. ## Environment variables | Variable | Default | Purpose | |----------|---------|---------| | `DATABASE_URL` | `postgres://localhost:5432/changelogue?sslmode=disable` | PostgreSQL connection | | `LISTEN_ADDR` | `:8080` | HTTP server bind address | | `NO_AUTH` | _(unset)_ | Set to `true` to disable API key auth (development) | | `LOG_LEVEL` | `info` | Log level: `debug`, `info`, `warn`, `error` | | `LLM_PROVIDER` | `gemini` | LLM provider: `gemini` or `openai` | | `LLM_MODEL` | `gemini-2.5-flash` / `gpt-5.2` | Model name (default depends on provider) | | `GOOGLE_API_KEY` | _(empty)_ | Gemini API key (required when `LLM_PROVIDER=gemini`) | | `OPENAI_API_KEY` | _(empty)_ | OpenAI API key (required when `LLM_PROVIDER=openai`) | | `OPENAI_BASE_URL` | `https://api.openai.com/v1` | OpenAI-compatible API base URL | ## Project structure ``` cmd/ server/ Entry point — wires all layers together agent/ Agent CLI — run agent analysis for a project internal/ agent/ ADK-Go agent orchestrator, tools, and worker openai/ OpenAI-compatible LLM provider api/ REST API, SSE, middleware, auth db/ Connection pool and migrations ingestion/ Polling sources (Docker Hub, GitHub, ECR Public, GitLab, PyPI) models/ Shared domain types queue/ River job definitions and client routing/ Notification channels and delivery worker web/ Next.js dashboard (React + Tailwind + shadcn) scripts/ Integration test harness ``` ## Extending More providers (npm, Helm, etc.) and channels (PagerDuty, etc.) are planned. Adding one is a single-interface implementation: **Add a registry provider** — implement `IIngestionSource` in `internal/ingestion/source.go`: ```go type IIngestionSource interface { FetchNewReleases(ctx context.Context) ([]IngestionResult, error) } ``` **Add a notification channel** — implement `Sender` in `internal/routing/sender.go`: ```go type Sender interface { Send(ctx context.Context, channel models.NotificationChannel, msg Message) error } ``` ## Useful commands ```bash make build # go build -o changelogue ./cmd/server make test # go test ./... make vet # go vet ./... make lint # alias for vet make integration-test # full integration test (spins up its own Postgres) make db-reset # drop and recreate the local database make frontend-build # build Next.js static export make agent-dev # run agent CLI for a specific project make clean # remove binary, stop containers, delete volumes ```

toolUseResult.originalFile:
.PHONY: up down db-reset build run dev test vet lint \
        frontend-install frontend-dev frontend-build \
        integration-test agent-dev clean

# --- Configuration ---
DATABASE_URL ?= postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/changelogue?sslmode=disable
LISTEN_ADDR  ?= :8080
BINARY       := changelogue

# --- Infrastructure ---
up:
	docker compose up -d
	@echo "Waiting for Postgres to be healthy..."
	@until docker compose exec -T postgres pg_isready -U postgres >/dev/null 2>&1; do sleep 1; done
	@echo "Postgres is ready."

down:
	docker compose down

db-reset:
	docker compose exec -T postgres psql -U postgres -c "DROP DATABASE IF EXISTS changelogue;"
	docker compose exec -T postgres psql -U postgres -c "CREATE DATABASE changelogue;"
	@echo "Database reset."

# --- Backend ---
build:
	go build -o $(BINARY) ./cmd/server

run: build
	DATABASE_URL="$(DATABASE_URL)" LISTEN_ADDR="$(LISTEN_ADDR)" NO_AUTH=true ./$(BINARY)

test:
	go test ./...

vet:
	go vet ./...

lint: vet

# --- Agent Dev ---
agent-dev:
	@if [ -z "$(PROJECT_ID)" ]; then echo "error: PROJECT_ID is required. Usage: make agent-dev PROJECT_ID=<uuid>"; exit 1; fi
	DATABASE_URL="$(DATABASE_URL)" go run ./cmd/agent --project-id=$(PROJECT_ID) web api webui

# --- Frontend ---
frontend-install:
	cd web && npm install

frontend-dev:
	cd web && npm run dev

frontend-build:
	cd web && npm run build

# --- Integration ---
integration-test:
	bash scripts/integration-test.sh

# --- Convenience ---
dev: up run

clean:
	rm -f $(BINARY)
	docker compose down -v

lines: -.PHONY: up down db-reset build run dev test vet lint \
+.PHONY: up down db-reset build run dev test vet lint coverage \

	go tool cover -func=coverage.out | grep total

file.content:
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, master]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main, master]

jobs:
  backend:
    name: Backend
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: actions/setup-go@v5
        with:
          go-version-file: go.mod

      - name: Vet
        run: go vet ./...

      - name: Build
        run: go build -o changelogue ./cmd/server

      - name: Test
        run: go test -coverprofile=coverage.out ./...

      - name: Coverage badge
        if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && github.event_name == 'push'
        run: |
          COV=$(go tool cover -func=coverage.out | grep total | awk '{print substr($3, 1, length($3)-1)}')
          echo "COVERAGE=$COV" >> $GITHUB_ENV

      - name: Update coverage badge
        if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && github.event_name == 'push'
        uses: Schneegans/dynamic-badges-action@v1.7.0
        with:
          auth: ${{ secrets.GIST_SECRET }}
          gistID: 94cc8f0ddf90bb1d04dafbb76102786d
          filename: coverage.json
          label: coverage
          message: ${{ env.COVERAGE }}%
          valColorRange: ${{ env.COVERAGE }}
          minColorRange: 20
          maxColorRange: 80

  frontend:
    name: Frontend
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    defaults:
      run:
        working-directory: web
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: npm
          cache-dependency-path: web/package-lock.json

      - name: Install
        run: npm ci

      - name: Lint
        run: npx eslint

      - name: Build
        run: npm run build

  integration:
    name: Integration
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: actions/setup-go@v5
        with:
          go-version-file: go.mod

      - name: Run integration tests
        run: bash scripts/integration-test.sh

.PHONY: up down db-reset build run dev test vet lint coverage \
        frontend-install frontend-dev frontend-build \
        integration-test agent-dev clean

# --- Configuration ---
DATABASE_URL ?= postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/changelogue?sslmode=disable
LISTEN_ADDR  ?= :8080
BINARY       := changelogue

# --- Infrastructure ---
up:
	docker compose up -d
	@echo "Waiting for Postgres to be healthy..."
	@until docker compose exec -T postgres pg_isready -U postgres >/dev/null 2>&1; do sleep 1; done
	@echo "Postgres is ready."

down:
	docker compose down

db-reset:
	docker compose exec -T postgres psql -U postgres -c "DROP DATABASE IF EXISTS changelogue;"
	docker compose exec -T postgres psql -U postgres -c "CREATE DATABASE changelogue;"
	@echo "Database reset."

# --- Backend ---
build:
	go build -o $(BINARY) ./cmd/server

run: build
	DATABASE_URL="$(DATABASE_URL)" LISTEN_ADDR="$(LISTEN_ADDR)" NO_AUTH=true ./$(BINARY)

test:
	go test ./...

coverage:
	go test -coverprofile=coverage.out ./...
	go tool cover -func=coverage.out | grep total

vet:
	go vet ./...

lint: vet

# --- Agent Dev ---
agent-dev:
	@if [ -z "$(PROJECT_ID)" ]; then echo "error: PROJECT_ID is required. Usage: make agent-dev PROJECT_ID=<uuid>"; exit 1; fi
	DATABASE_URL="$(DATABASE_URL)" go run ./cmd/agent --project-id=$(PROJECT_ID) web api webui

# --- Frontend ---
frontend-install:
	cd web && npm install

frontend-dev:
	cd web && npm run dev

frontend-build:
	cd web && npm run build

# --- Integration ---
integration-test:
	bash scripts/integration-test.sh

# --- Convenience ---
dev: up run

clean:
	rm -f $(BINARY)
	docker compose down -v

research tools"] D -.- D1["Slack · Discord
Email · Webhooks"] ``` Release insert and job enqueue happen in a single SQL transaction — zero-loss guarantee. See [ARCH.md](ARCH.md), [API.md](API.md), and [DESIGN.md](DESIGN.md) for the full design. ## Quick start **Prerequisites:** Go 1.25+, Docker, Node.js 20+ ```bash # Start Postgres and the server (NO_AUTH mode) make dev # In another terminal — start the frontend make frontend-install make frontend-dev ``` The API runs on `localhost:8080`, the dashboard on `localhost:3000`. ## Environment variables | Variable | Default | Purpose | |----------|---------|---------| | `DATABASE_URL` | `postgres://localhost:5432/changelogue?sslmode=disable` | PostgreSQL connection | | `LISTEN_ADDR` | `:8080` | HTTP server bind address | | `NO_AUTH` | _(unset)_ | Set to `true` to disable API key auth (development) | | `LOG_LEVEL` | `info` | Log level: `debug`, `info`, `warn`, `error` | | `LLM_PROVIDER` | `gemini` | LLM provider: `gemini` or `openai` | | `LLM_MODEL` | `gemini-2.5-flash` / `gpt-5.2` | Model name (default depends on provider) | | `GOOGLE_API_KEY` | _(empty)_ | Gemini API key (required when `LLM_PROVIDER=gemini`) | | `OPENAI_API_KEY` | _(empty)_ | OpenAI API key (required when `LLM_PROVIDER=openai`) | | `OPENAI_BASE_URL` | `https://api.openai.com/v1` | OpenAI-compatible API base URL | ## Project structure ``` cmd/ server/ Entry point — wires all layers together agent/ Agent CLI — run agent analysis for a project internal/ agent/ ADK-Go agent orchestrator, tools, and worker openai/ OpenAI-compatible LLM provider api/ REST API, SSE, middleware, auth db/ Connection pool and migrations ingestion/ Polling sources (Docker Hub, GitHub, ECR Public, GitLab, PyPI) models/ Shared domain types queue/ River job definitions and client routing/ Notification channels and delivery worker web/ Next.js dashboard (React + Tailwind + shadcn) scripts/ Integration test harness ``` ## Extending More providers (npm, Helm, etc.) and channels (PagerDuty, etc.) are planned. Adding one is a single-interface implementation: **Add a registry provider** — implement `IIngestionSource` in `internal/ingestion/source.go`: ```go type IIngestionSource interface { FetchNewReleases(ctx context.Context) ([]IngestionResult, error) } ``` **Add a notification channel** — implement `Sender` in `internal/routing/sender.go`: ```go type Sender interface { Send(ctx context.Context, channel models.NotificationChannel, msg Message) error } ``` ## Useful commands ```bash make build # go build -o changelogue ./cmd/server make test # go test ./... make vet # go vet ./... make lint # alias for vet make integration-test # full integration test (spins up its own Postgres) make db-reset # drop and recreate the local database make frontend-build # build Next.js static export make agent-dev # run agent CLI for a specific project make clean # remove binary, stop containers, delete volumes ```

content.text:
All changes are in place. Here's a summary:

**`.github/workflows/ci.yml`** — The `Test` step now generates a coverage profile (`-coverprofile=coverage.out`). Two new steps (gated on `main` push only) parse the total coverage % and update the gist badge via `Schneegans/dynamic-badges-action@v1.7.0`. The color ranges from red (20%) to green (80%).

**`README.md`** — Added a `![Coverage]` badge next to the existing AI Co-Authored badge, pointing to the same gist. Also documented `make coverage` in the useful commands section.

**`Makefile`** — Added a `coverage` target that runs tests with `-coverprofile` and prints the total line.

To verify locally, run `make coverage` — it will print the total coverage percentage.

content.content:
diff --git a/.github/workflows/ci.yml b/.github/workflows/ci.yml
index ceed21a..fb8dffe 100644
--- a/.github/workflows/ci.yml
+++ b/.github/workflows/ci.yml
@@ -24,7 +24,26 @@ jobs:
         run: go build -o changelogue ./cmd/server
 
       - name: Test
-        run: go test ./...
+        run: go test -coverprofile=coverage.out ./...
+
+      - name: Coverage badge
+        if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && github.event_name == 'push'
+        run: |
+          COV=$(go tool cover -func=coverage.out | grep total | awk '{print substr($3, 1, length($3)-1)}')
+          echo "COVERAGE=$COV" >> $GITHUB_ENV
+
+      - name: Update coverage badge
+        if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && github.event_name == 'push'
+        uses: Schneegans/dynamic-badges-action@v1.7.0
+        with:
+          auth: ${{ secrets.GIST_SECRET }}
+          gistID: 94cc8f0ddf90bb1d04dafbb76102786d
+          filename: coverage.json
+          label: coverage
+          message: ${{ env.COVERAGE }}%
+          valColorRange: ${{ env.COVERAGE }}
+          minColorRange: 20
+          maxColorRange: 80
 
   frontend:
     name: Frontend
diff --git a/Makefile b/Makefile
index 682b3ad..408f99d 100644
--- a/Makefile
+++ b/Makefile
@@ -1,4 +1,4 @@
-.PHONY: up down db-reset build run dev test vet lint \
+.PHONY: up down db-reset build run dev test vet lint coverage \
         frontend-install frontend-dev frontend-build \
         integration-test agent-dev clean
 
@@ -32,6 +32,10 @@ run: build
 test:
 	go test ./...
 
+coverage:
+	go test -coverprofile=coverage.out ./...
+	go tool cover -func=coverage.out | grep total
+
 vet:
 	go vet ./...
 
diff --git a/README.md b/README.md
index 83b0cef..18f9336 100644
--- a/README.md
+++ b/README.md
@@ -4,6 +4,7 @@
 
 ![Build](https://github.com/sentioxyz/releaseguard/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)
 ![AI Co-Authored](https://img.shields.io/endpoint?url=https://gist.githubusercontent.com/Poytr1/94cc8f0ddf90bb1d04dafbb76102786d/raw/ai-commits.json)
+![Coverage](https://img.shields.io/endpoint?url=https://gist.githubusercontent.com/Poytr1/94cc8f0ddf90bb1d04dafbb76102786d/raw/coverage.json)
 
 ## What it does
 
@@ -103,6 +104,7 @@ type Sender interface {
 ```bash
 make build              # go build -o changelogue ./cmd/server
 make test               # go test ./...
+make coverage           # go test with coverage profile + print total %
 make vet                # go vet ./...
 make lint               # alias for vet
 make integration-test   # full integration test (spins up its own Postgres)

git add .github/workflows/ci.yml README.md Makefile && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(ci): add auto-updating code coverage badge

Generate coverage profile in CI, parse total %, and update a gist-backed
shields.io badge on pushes to main. Add local `make coverage` target.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git status
